title: Count the Number of Beautiful Subarrays

testcases.input: nums = [4,3,1,2,4] Output: 2 Explanation: There are 2 beautiful subarrays in nums: [4,3,1,2,4] and [4,3,1,2,4]. - We can make all elements in the subarray [3,1,2] equal to 0 in the following way: - Choose [3, 1, 2] and k = 1. Subtract 21 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [1, 1, 0]. - Choose [1, 1, 0] and k = 0. Subtract 20 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 0, 0]. - We can make all elements in the subarray [4,3,1,2,4] equal to 0 in the following way: - Choose [4, 3, 1, 2, 4] and k = 2. Subtract 22 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 3, 1, 2, 0]. - Choose [0, 3, 1, 2, 0] and k = 0. Subtract 20 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 2, 0, 2, 0]. - Choose [0, 2, 0, 2, 0] and k = 1. Subtract 21 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 0, 0, 0, 0].
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: There are 2 beautiful subarrays in nums: [4,3,1,2,4] and [4,3,1,2,4]. - We can make all elements in the subarray [3,1,2] equal to 0 in the following way: - Choose [3, 1, 2] and k = 1. Subtract 21 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [1, 1, 0]. - Choose [1, 1, 0] and k = 0. Subtract 20 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 0, 0]. - We can make all elements in the subarray [4,3,1,2,4] equal to 0 in the following way: - Choose [4, 3, 1, 2, 4] and k = 2. Subtract 22 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 3, 1, 2, 0]. - Choose [0, 3, 1, 2, 0] and k = 0. Subtract 20 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 2, 0, 2, 0]. - Choose [0, 2, 0, 2, 0] and k = 1. Subtract 21 from both numbers. The subarray becomes [0, 0, 0, 0, 0].

testcases.input: nums = [1,10,4] Output: 0 Explanation: There are no beautiful subarrays in nums.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: There are no beautiful subarrays in nums.
